keyPointsSummary: 'Would' is used for polite requests, past habits, and hypothetical situations
'Ought to' expresses moral duty or what is correct, similar to 'should' but stronger in written English
'Dare' expresses having the courage to do something risky
Negative: would not, ought not to, dare not

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं पहले हर रोज़ वहाँ जाता था।
quickQuiz.answer: I would go there every day before.

quickQuiz.hindi: हमें अपने माता-पिता की इज्जत करनी चाहिए।
quickQuiz.answer: We ought to respect our parents.
You ought to tell the truth.

quickQuiz.hindi: उसकी हिम्मत नहीं है ऐसा कहने की।
quickQuiz.answer: He does not dare to say this.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं यह पहले नहीं करता था।
quickQuiz.answer: I would not do this before.

quickQuiz.hindi: हमें नियमों का पालन करना चाहिए।
quickQuiz.answer: We ought to follow the rules.

quickQuiz.hindi: क्या तुम्हें उससे बात करने की हिम्मत है?
quickQuiz.answer: Do you dare to talk to him?

mustRememberRules: 'Would' is used for polite requests, past habits, and hypothetical situations
'Ought to' expresses moral duty or what is correct, similar to 'should' but stronger in written English
'Dare' expresses having the courage to do something risky
Negative: would not, ought not to, dare not
